title: Top 5 Iconic Hotel Room Scenes in Hollywood Movies
author: Amots Yanai
excerpt: From moments of sheer hilarity to deep emotional connections, some of the most iconic scenes in film history have taken place in these transient spaces. So, grab your complimentary robe and minibar snack as we check into the top five iconic hotel room scenes in Hollywood movies.
body:
If you've ever stayed in a hotel, you know they can be the setting for some pretty memorable moments. Hollywood knows this too, and over the years, filmmakers have taken full advantage of the unique atmosphere that hotel rooms provide. From moments of sheer hilarity to deep emotional connections, some of the most iconic scenes in film history have taken place in these transient spaces. So, grab your complimentary robe and minibar snack as we check into the top five iconic hotel room scenes in Hollywood movies. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 1. The Shining (1980) Гўв‚¬вЂњ Room 237

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Kicking things off with a scene that has haunted audiences for decades, we have The Shining. If thereГўв‚¬в„ўs a list of hotel rooms, this film needs to be on it. Stanley KubrickГўв‚¬в„ўs adaptation of Stephen King's novel is legendary, and the infamous Room 237 scene is a big reason why. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Jack Torrance, played by the ever-intense Jack Nicholson, has been going slowly mad while caring for the Overlook Hotel during the off-season. When his son, Danny, wanders into Room 237, he encounters visions that are nothing short of terrifying. Jack himself later enters the room and experiences a surreal, horrifying encounter with a ghostly woman who transforms from alluring to decaying in a matter of seconds. The sceneГўв‚¬в„ўs perfect blend of psychological horror and stunning visuals solidifies its place in cinema history. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 2. Pretty Woman (1990) Гўв‚¬вЂњ The Bathtub Scene

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

On a much lighter note, we move to the beloved romantic comedy Pretty Woman. The film follows the unlikely romance between wealthy businessman Edward Lewis (Richard Gere) and vivacious sex worker Vivian Ward (Julia Roberts). Their relationship blossoms in the luxurious penthouse suite of the Beverly Wilshire Hotel.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

One of the most iconic scenes is when Vivian, after a shopping spree, sings her heart out to PrinceГўв‚¬в„ўs Гўв‚¬Е“KissГўв‚¬Вќ while soaking in a bubble bath. The moment is charming, carefree, and perfectly captures Vivian's transformation and newfound confidence. Julia Roberts' infectious energy makes it impossible not to smile as she belts out the lyrics, bubbles up to her neck. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 3. Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992) Гўв‚¬вЂњ The Plaza Hotel

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Who could forget Kevin McCallisterГўв‚¬в„ўs adventures in Home Alone 2: Lost in New York? When young Kevin (Macaulay Culkin) finds himself accidentally separated from his family once again, he checks into the lavish Plaza Hotel using his dadГўв‚¬в„ўs credit card. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

The best scene? Undoubtedly, the one where Kevin encounters the suspicious hotel staff, including a hilarious Tim Curry as the concierge. Using the hotelГўв‚¬в„ўs services to their fullest, Kevin manages to outsmart the staff with the help of a movie recording, culminating in the famous Гўв‚¬Е“Merry Christmas, ya filthy animalГўв‚¬Вќ line. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs a delightful mix of wit, humor, and the sheer joy of a kid living out a fantasy in one of New YorkГўв‚¬в„ўs most iconic hotels.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 4. Lost in Translation (2003) Гўв‚¬вЂњ The Park Hyatt Tokyo

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Sofia Coppola's Lost in Translation is a masterclass in depicting loneliness and connection in a foreign land. The film stars Bill Murray as aging actor Bob Harris and Scarlett Johansson as young college graduate Charlotte, who form an unlikely bond while staying at the Park Hyatt Tokyo. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

One of the film's most poignant scenes takes place in BobГўв‚¬в„ўs hotel room. After a long night of karaoke and exploring Tokyo, Bob and Charlotte return to his room, where they share a tender, hushed conversation. Lying on the bed, they talk about their fears, hopes, and the ennui of their lives. The intimacy and vulnerability of this scene, paired with the breathtaking view of TokyoГўв‚¬в„ўs skyline, create a moment that feels both deeply personal and universally relatable. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 5. Casino Royale (2006) Гўв‚¬вЂњ Hotel Splendide

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

James Bond and luxury hotels go together like martinis and olives, and Casino Royale delivers one of the best hotel scenes in the Bond franchise. After a particularly brutal fight, Bond (Daniel Craig) returns to his suite at Hotel Splendide in Montenegro. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Covered in blood and bruises, Bond sits in the shower, still wearing his tuxedo, where Vesper Lynd (Eva Green) joins him. The moment is raw and humanizing, showing a vulnerable side to the usually unflappable 007. Vesper's compassion and BondГўв‚¬в„ўs silent breakdown convey a depth of character rarely seen in action films. The scene is a stark reminder that even the most hardened spies need a moment to process their trauma. 

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

 Honorable Mentions

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

While these five scenes are standouts, there are plenty of other memorable hotel moments in film history. Here are a few honorable mentions that also deserve recognition:

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

- Psycho (1960) Гўв‚¬вЂњ The Bates Motel: Alfred HitchcockГўв‚¬в„ўs masterpiece brought us the terrifying Bates Motel and the unforgettable shower scene. While not exactly a "hotel room" scene, itГўв‚¬в„ўs too iconic to leave out entirely.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

- The Hangover (2009) Гўв‚¬вЂњ Caesars Palace: The aftermath of a wild night in Las Vegas unfolds hilariously in the suite of Caesars Palace, where the Wolf Pack tries to piece together what happened.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

- Ocean's Eleven (2001) Гўв‚¬вЂњ The Bellagio: George Clooney and his crew plan and execute one of the most stylish heists in cinematic history, with the Bellagio Hotel serving as their glamorous playground.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ

Hotels in movies serve as more than just backdrops; they become characters in their own right, adding depth, humor, and sometimes even horror to the stories. Whether itГўв‚¬в„ўs the Overlook Hotel's eerie corridors or the Beverly Wilshire's plush luxury, these scenes remind us of the unique and often pivotal role that hotel rooms play in film. So next time you check into a hotel, take a moment to appreciate the cinematic potential of your surroundingsГўв‚¬вЂќyou never know what iconic moment might be just around the corner.

Гўв‚¬ВЌ
